statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, determine if it is a subsequence of another array. The sequence can only be formed if elements are in order and without skipping any elements.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains the size of two arrays, and the second line contains the elements for the first array, and the third line contains the elements for the second array. Elements are separated by spaces.
statement.outputFormat: Output 'Yes' if the first array is a subsequence of the second; otherwise, output 'No'.
statement.notes: You can assume that both arrays are not empty and all elements are unique and positive.

hints.text: Understand the concept of subsequences and how to detect them. The solution involves tracking the index of elements in the first array and checking if they match the elements in the second array.

editorial.title: Subsequence Detection
editorial.intuition: The problem is a variation of finding a subsequence in an array. A subsequence is a sequence that can be derived from another sequence by deleting some elements without changing the order of the remaining elements.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution involves checking every possible subsequence of the second array, which is not an efficient approach.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution involves using two pointers to traverse both arrays. One pointer is used to track the current position in the first array, and the other pointer is used to track the current position in the second array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a similar approach but maintains the index of the current element in the first array and checks for a match in the second array.
commonMistakes: Mistaking two pointers for the same array
Not considering duplicate elements in the second array

functionSignature.cpp: bool isSubsequence(int n, int m, vector<int>& arr1, vector<int>& arr2);
functionSignature.java: boolean isSubsequence(int n, int m, int[] arr1, int[] arr2);
functionSignature.python: def isSubsequence(self, n: int, m: int, nums1: list[int], nums2: list[int]) -> bool:
functionSignature.javascript: function isSubsequence(n: int, m: int, arr1: Array<number>, arr2: Array<number>): boolean;
